name: Android MediaProjection Enhancements
project_status: Active platform API with Android 15 QPR1 behavior updates
capture_driver: System display or single app-window projection via virtual display
compose_support: Indirect; captures whatever the app renders, including Compose UI
view_support: Full display and single app window; app-sharing mode excludes system UI
stitching_strategy: None; streamed capture, not a stitching tool
confidence_fallback: Apps should handle onStop(), resize, and visibility callbacks
static_chrome_handling: App-screen sharing hides status bar, navigation bar, notifications, and other system UI; a status bar chip appears during sharing
dynamic_content_robustness: onCapturedContentResize() and onCapturedContentVisibilityChanged() help handle rotation, multi-window, and visibility changes; projection auto-stops when locked
content_type_fitness: Best for live app or display sharing, not long screenshot stitching
output_format: Raw screen frames to a Surface/VirtualDisplay
oem_compatibility: Behavior is platform-defined; Android 15 QPR1 adds the prominent status bar chip and auto-stop on lock
integration: Framework API plus manifest foreground service type=mediaProjection and user-consent intent
media_projection_compliance: Requires FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PROJECTION, one token per session, one createVirtualDisplay() per token, status chip, and auto-stop on lock
windowing_behavior: Supports full display or a selected app window; resize callbacks keep aspect ratio correct across rotation and windowing changes
data_path: Fully on-device unless the app forwards the stream elsewhere
permissions_required: User consent via createScreenCaptureIntent(); FOREGROUND_SERVICE and FOREGROUND_SERVICE_MEDIA_PROJECTION
